term_name: ABB Commercial Code
display_name: ABB Commercial Code
description: Code for deviating power of disposal used in case of blocking an account.
notes: Also known as ABB codes.

term_name: ABB Operational Code
display_name: ABB Operational Code
description: Code for deviating power of disposal used in case of blocking financial transactions.
notes: Also known as ABB codes.

term_name: Account Description
display_name: Account Description
description: A line of text that can be printed on an information product. Often it contains the purpose of the acccount.

term_name: Account IBAN
display_name: Account IBAN
description: The accountnumber in the International Bank Account Number (IBAN) format

term_name: Accounting Entry Account Currency Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Account Currency Code
description: Code of the currency of the accounting entry based on the currency of the account.

term_name: Accounting Entry Account Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Account Id
description: Identification of the account to which the accounting entry is related.
notes: This can be either the IBAN of the account (in case of a payment agreement) or the identification of a Group Settlement Agreement (GAO in Dutch) (in case of interest compensation/combination). The GOA id is a 6 digit number. The Basic Payment Product Number tells if it is an interest compensation (type = 88), an interest combination (type = 89) or a payment agreement (type <> 88/89).

term_name: Accounting Entry Account Servicer Reference Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Account Servicer Reference Id
description: Unique reference as assigned by the account servicing institution to identify the accounting entry.
notes: For entries replicated from Atlas, this field contains the RA or PA number. For entries replicated from COF, this field contains the transaction timestamp as assigned by COF.

term_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Balance Debit Credit Code
display_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Balance Debit Credit Code
description: Code to indicate whether the balance after booking is credit or a debit balance.
notes: Possible values: - CRDT = Credit, this means a positive balance for the customer. For the bank this is a debt. - DBIT = Debit, this means a negative balance or balance equal zero for the customer. For the bank this is an asset.

term_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Currency Balance
display_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Currency Balance
description: The book balance of the account after the specific accounting entry is posted, expressed in the currency of the related account.
notes: This is always a book balance.

term_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Currency Smallest Unit Balance
display_name: Accounting Entry After Booking Currency Smallest Unit Balance
description: The book balance of the account after the specific accounting entry is posted, expressed in the smallest unit of the currency of the related account.
notes: This is always a book balance

term_name: Accounting Entry Autonomous Tariff Group Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Autonomous Tariff Group Code
description: Code that refers to the autonomous tariff group that is used in the booking.
notes: Examples: BGP: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie BGZ: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie BSL: Beslaglegging BVA: Bankverklaring A BVB: Bankverklaring B BVS: Standaardbankverklaring COD: Coulance betaling COU: Coulance betaling CRD: Correctie creditrente CRE: Correctie creditrente DRD: Correctie debetrente DRE: Correctie debetrente KSH: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSL: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSS: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSX: Terugboeking schuldsanering

term_name: Accounting Entry Bank Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Bank Code
description: Code of the bank where the transaction is registered in the general ledger.
notes: This is the office number that was valid for the corresponding agreement of the transaction at the time of execution. The time of execution is based on the transaction control timestamp assigned to the transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry Bank Message Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Bank Message Id
description: Unique identification, as assigned by the bank, to unambiguously identify a message, related to the accounting entry
notes: The message is technically a file. The identification is for bank internal (traceability) use only, and may therefore not be used in customer reporting.

term_name: Accounting Entry Bank Payment Information Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Bank Payment Information Id
description: Unique identification, as assigned by the bank, to unambiguously identify the paymentbatch, related to the accounting entry.
notes: The identification is for bank internal (traceability) use only, and may therefore not be used in customer reporting.

term_name: Accounting Entry Basic Payment Product Number
display_name: Accounting Entry Basic Payment Product Number
description: Unique number to identify the basic payment product which the transaction is part of.
notes: Code (number) indicating a product related to a Basic Payment Agreement (BBO). Also called 'accounttype' . This is the Basic Payment Product Number that was valid for the corresponding agreement of the transaction at the time of execution. The time of execution is based on the transaction control timestamp assigned to the transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry Book Date
display_name: Accounting Entry Book Date
description: Date on which the accounting entry is to be posted in the general ledger.
notes: Date used for General Ledger and Customer reporting. Book Date is always a business day-date (not a date during a weekend or bank holiday). Booking Date closure within bank is currently around 7:30 pm CET/CEST. Book Date is generated (with the first not closed Booking Date) at the moment an accounting entry is posted to an account in the Current Account administration.

term_name: Accounting Entry Contra Account IBAN
display_name: Accounting Entry Contra Account IBAN
description: The IBAN of the account of the contra booking.

term_name: Accounting Entry Contra Account Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Contra Account Id
description: The id of the account of the contra booking.
notes: This is either a BBAN or an IBAN

term_name: Accounting Entry Credit Management Commission Group Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Credit Management Commission Group Code
description: Code that refers to the Credit Management commission group that is used in the booking.

term_name: Accounting Entry Currency Amount
display_name: Accounting Entry Currency Amount
description: Booking amount of the accounting entry in the currency of the account related to the accounting entry.

term_name: Accounting Entry Currency Number Of Decimals
display_name: Accounting Entry Currency Number Of Decimals
description: The number of decimals in which the currency of the accounting entry is expressed.
notes: This is based on the currency of the event.

term_name: Accounting Entry Currency Smallest Unit Amount
display_name: Accounting Entry Currency Smallest Unit Amount
description: Booking amount of the Accounting Entry in the smallest unit of the currency of the account related to the accounting entry.

term_name: Accounting Entry Debit Credit Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Debit Credit Code
description: Indication whether the accounting entry is a debit or a credit entry.
notes: Possible values: - CRDT = Credit, a booking with this value results in an increase of the balance of the customer - DBIT = Debit, a booking with this value results in a decrease of the balance of the customer

term_name: Accounting Entry Delivery Channel Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Delivery Channel Code
description: Identification of the distribution channel or product administration through which the transaction, related to the accounting entry is initiated or received by bank.
notes: For instance: RIB, RDC or ART.

term_name: Accounting Entry Detailed Transaction Type Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Detailed Transaction Type Code
description: Code to indicate the detailed type of the financial transaction, related to the accounting entry.
notes: Further detailing of Transaction Type, used among others, for billing. In principle only for internal usage. Example: detailed transaction type code 23 stands for 'opname geldautomaat niet-' within transaction group 'GA - Geldautomaat'

term_name: Accounting Entry End-To-End Id
display_name: Accounting Entry End-To-End Id
description: Unique identification, as assigned by the initiating party, to identify the transaction, related to the accounting entry
notes: This identification is passed on (unchanged) throughout the entire end-to-end chain.

term_name: Accounting Entry First Remittance Information Description
display_name: Accounting Entry First Remittance Information Description
description: Free format remittance information description position 1 through 25.

term_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Event Code
display_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Event Code
description: Code that represents the general ledger accounting entry event.
domain_values: General Ledger event code (Finance is owner)
notes: e.g. COST, BTW

term_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Event Currency Code
display_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Event Currency Code
description: ISO standard 4217 (3 characters) Currency Code of the amount of the general ledger event of an accounting entry.
notes: For some general ledger events the currency of the amount of an accounting entry is different than the currency of the related agreement. The value of the currency code is based on the ISO standard 4217 (3 characters).

term_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: Code of the financial entity where the transaction is registered in the general ledger.
notes: This is the Financial Entity that was valid for the corresponding agreement of the transaction at the time of execution. The time of execution is based on the transaction control timestamp assigned to the transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: Accounting Entry General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: Code of the operating unit where the transaction is registered in the general ledger.
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)
notes: This is the Operating Unit that was valid for the corresponding agreement of the transaction at the time of execution. The time of execution is based on the transaction control timestamp assigned to the transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry Origin Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Origin Code
description: A unique code indicating which system delivered the booking.
notes: This field is used for GL entries and is filled with the origin (source). e.g. OFI1, ART1

term_name: Accounting Entry Payment Account Currency Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Payment Account Currency Code
description: Code of the currency of the accounting entry based on the currency of the payment account.
notes: ISO 3 standard,' 3-lettercode'

term_name: Accounting Entry Payment Account IBAN
display_name: Accounting Entry Payment Account IBAN
description: IBAN of the payment account on which the accounting entry is posted.

term_name: Accounting Entry Period End Date
display_name: Accounting Entry Period End Date
description: The end date of the period to which the accounting entry is related.

term_name: Accounting Entry Period Start Date
display_name: Accounting Entry Period Start Date
description: The start date of the period to which the accounting entry is related.

term_name: Accounting Entry Product Number
display_name: Accounting Entry Product Number
description: The unique identifier for a product where the accounting entry is related to in the General Ledger.
notes: This is a general field for a product number. This is not meant for a specific product. It can be a payment product, a savings product etc.

term_name: Accounting Entry Related Contract Number
display_name: Accounting Entry Related Contract Number
description: Unique number of the contract where the posting is related to.
notes: Optional field. Reference number for specific General ledger bookings. No fixed value.

term_name: Accounting Entry Related Institution Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Related Institution Code
description: The unique id of the related institution where the transaction is registered in the general ledger
notes: This is the affiliated instition identification that was valid for the corresponding agreement of the transaction at the time of execution. The time of execution is based on the transaction control timestamp assigned to the transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry Second Remittance Information Description
display_name: Accounting Entry Second Remittance Information Description
description: Free format remittance information description position 26 through 50.

term_name: Accounting Entry Sequence Number
display_name: Accounting Entry Sequence Number
description: Unique number for an entry for an account.
notes: Unique sequence number for one agreement, increasing by 1 for every booking.

term_name: Accounting Entry Settlement Account Currency Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Settlement Account Currency Code
description: ISO standard 4217 (3 characters) code of the currency of the account which is used to pay the RPK settlement.

term_name: Accounting Entry Settlement Account IBAN
display_name: Accounting Entry Settlement Account IBAN
description: The IBAN of the account which is used to pay the RPK settlement.

term_name: Accounting Entry Tariff Group Number Of Units
display_name: Accounting Entry Tariff Group Number Of Units
description: The number of units purchased from a bank service by the customer.

term_name: Accounting Entry Tax Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Tax Code
description: Code that refers to a specific tax rate.
notes: H: BTW hoog tarief, L: BTW laag tarief, X: Nul tarief

term_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Account Servicer Reference Id
display_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Account Servicer Reference Id
description: Unique reference as assigned by the account servicing insitution (bank belonging to the account) to identify the instruction, related to the accounting entry
notes: This identification will always be assigned by bank and will be the unique reference for Controlability purposes ('Controleerbaarheid')

term_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Control Datetime
display_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Control Datetime
description: Date and time of processing, given by the Payment Factory to a transaction, related to the accounting entry in order to check the accuracy and completeness of payments
notes: This timestamp is used to guarantee timeliness, correctness and completeness of the processed transaction.

term_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Type Code
display_name: Accounting Entry Transaction Type Code
description: Two character code specifying the type of financial transaction, related to the accounting entry.
notes: This is used, among others, for the representation on account statements. Example: transaction group 'GA' stand for 'Geldautomaat'.

term_name: Accounting Entry Value Date
display_name: Accounting Entry Value Date
description: Date at which the accounting entry becomes interest bearing.

term_name: Accounting Entry Year Month
display_name: Accounting Entry Year Month
description: The year and month of the booking date of the accounting entry.
notes: Format: YYYYMM

term_name: Account Number
display_name: Account Number
description: Identifier used nationally by financial institutions, ie, in individual countries, generally as part of a National Account Numbering Scheme(s), to uniquely identify the account of a customer.
notes: Synonym: BBAN

term_name: Account Status Code
display_name: Account Status Code
description: Code indicating the status of the payment account (basic payment agreement).
notes: A: Actief,N: Opgeheven maar nog financieel af te handelen,O: Opgeheven en financieel afgewikkeld

term_name: Actual Book Balance
display_name: Actual Book Balance
description: The total amount in euro of all entries on an account until the moment of the request.

term_name: Address Counter Account Desired Flag
display_name: Address Counter Account Desired Flag
description: An indicator that comprises if the address and domicile (as far as available) have to added to the informationproduct.
notes: J: Ja, N: Nee

term_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Agreement Location Code
display_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Agreement Location Code
description: The code of the administrative location (main office, branch office, counter) on which the local bank wants information about the account such as reports to be registered, after the change
notes: The location code account can be an identifier for: - a location of a local bank - a branch of a local bank - a department within a branch - a special group. The local banks decide for themselves what location codes they want to use. The management of these codes is therefore entirely the responsibility of the local banks and this information is not known to the central systems. The central systems can therefore not carry out any checks on them. The default value is '00'. There is no direct relationship between the location code account and the local bank location.

term_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Agreement  Status Code
display_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Agreement  Status Code
description: Code that indicates the status of a basic payment agreement after a mutation.

term_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Product Number
display_name: After Mutation Basic Payment Product Number
description: The identifying number of the product and associated product conditions which are agreed with the customer, after the change
notes: Code (number) indicating a product related to a Basic Payment Agreement (BBO). Also called 'accounttype'

term_name: After Mutation General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: After Mutation General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity in the General ledger under which the bookings concerning the Account agreement is reported, after the change.

term_name: After Mutation General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: After Mutation General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: The code in the General ledger under which the Account agreement related Profit-loss is managed, after the change
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)

term_name: After Mutation Office Number
display_name: After Mutation Office Number
description: The unique identifier for a bank institution, after the change

term_name: After Mutation Related Institution Id
display_name: After Mutation Related Institution Id
description: The unique code of institutions / companies (within the bank Group) for which local banks act as an intermediate, after the change

term_name: Agreement Discount Arrangement End Date
display_name: Agreement Discount Arrangement End Date
description: The last date a discount arrangement is valid for an agreement

term_name: Agreement Discount Arrangement Start Date
display_name: Agreement Discount Arrangement Start Date
description: The first date a discount arrangement is valid for an agreement

term_name: Amount Currency Code
display_name: Amount Currency Code
description: 3-letter currency code according to ISO standard 4217

term_name: APO Beneficiary Name
display_name: APO Beneficiary Name
description: Name of the beneficiary as raised by the debtor of the APO

term_name: APO Contract Change Datetime
display_name: APO Contract Change Datetime
description: Date and timestamp of latest received modification to the APO agreement by bank initiated by customer

term_name: APO End Date
display_name: APO End Date
description: Date that the payment transaction as a result of the APO will be offered for the last time to the payment engine

term_name: APO Id
display_name: APO Id
description: Unique identification of the APO

term_name: APO Modify Code
display_name: APO Modify Code
description: Code that indicaties the kind of modification that is delivered in the dataset with APO data
notes: I = New APO,U = Modified APO,D = Deleted APO

term_name: APO Mutation Datetime
display_name: APO Mutation Datetime
description: Date and timestamp of a mutation on the APO made by customer, DBA or system.

term_name: APO Next Execution Date
display_name: APO Next Execution Date
description: Date that the payment transaction as a result of the APO has to be offered to the payment engine next time

term_name: APO Number Of Executions
display_name: APO Number Of Executions
description: Number of times the payment transaction as a result of the APO has to be executed

term_name: APO Period Unit Code
display_name: APO Period Unit Code
description: Entity indicating the period for repeating the execution of the APO
notes: Allowed values: w (week), m( month) and y (year)

term_name: APO Period Unit Number
display_name: APO Period Unit Number
description: Quantity of repetition period expression to be used for calculation of the APO next execution date
domain_values: Allowed values in combination with field

term_name: APO Rounding Code
display_name: APO Rounding Code
description: Code indicating how to round the amount of the APO.
domain_values:
0 - No rounding 
1 - Rounding to €        1,00 
2 - Rounding to €        2,00
3 - Rounding to €        5,00
4 - Rounding to €      50,00
5 - Rounding to €    100,00
6 - Rounding to € 1.000,00

term_name: APO Saldo Sparen Maximum Transaction Euro Amount
display_name: APO Saldo Sparen Maximum Transaction Euro Amount
description: The maximum amount in Euro allowed for generated Saldo Sparen APO transaction

term_name: APO Saldo Sparen Minimum Account Euro Balance
display_name: APO Saldo Sparen Minimum Account Euro Balance
description: The minimum balance in Euro which should be available on the current account after executing a Saldo Sparen payment instruction

term_name: APO Saldo Sparen Minimum Transaction Euro Amount
display_name: APO Saldo Sparen Minimum Transaction Euro Amount
description: The minimum amount in Euro allowed for generated Saldo Sparen APO transaction

term_name: APO Status Code
display_name: APO Status Code
description: State of the APO agreement during the life cycle.
notes: Legal values: ,1 - To be verified,2 - To be released,3 - Active,5 - Rejected,9 - Ended

term_name: APO Type Code
display_name: APO Type Code
description: Code indicating the APO type.
notes: 00 = normale APO, 01 = Saldo sparen APO

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Additional Description
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Additional Description
description: The additional description of the autonomous detailed transaction type.

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Code
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Code
description: The unique code of the autonomous detailed transaction type.
notes: Synonym: Autonomous Tariff Group. e.g. BGP: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie,BGZ: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Dutch Description
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Dutch Description
description: The description of the autonomous detailed transaction type in Dutch.

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type End Date
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type End Date
description: The last date of the period in which the autonomous detailed transaction type is valid.
notes: Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type English Description
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type English Description
description: The description of the autonomous detailed transaction type in English.

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Interest Debit Credit Code
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Interest Debit Credit Code
description: Code that indicates if the interest is a debit or a credit amount, in case the autonomous detailed transaction type concerns a correction of interest.
notes: D=Debit, C=Credit. Only applicable if the RPK Code is 'R'.

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Presentation Cluster Code
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Presentation Cluster Code
description: The unique code of the presentation cluster the autonomous detailed transaction type belongs to.

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type RPK Code
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type RPK Code
description: The code that indicates to which RPK-group the autonomous detailed transaction type belongs.
notes: RPK: R = Interest P = Commission K = Costs

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Start Date
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type Start Date
description: The first date of the period in which the autonomous detailed transaction type is valid.
notes: Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD

term_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type VAT Code
display_name: Autonomous Detailed Transaction Type VAT Code
description: The code identifying the VAT tariff that must be used for the autonomous detailed transaction type.
notes: H: BTW hoog tarief, L: BTW laag tarief, X: Nul tarief

term_name: Balance Account Currency Code
display_name: Balance Account Currency Code
description: Code of the currency of the balance based on the currency of the account.

term_name: Balance Account Id
display_name: Balance Account Id
description: Identification of the account to which the balance is related.
notes: This can be an IBAN (in case of payment agreement) or some other id (GAO).

term_name: Balance Autonomous Tariff Group Code
display_name: Balance Autonomous Tariff Group Code
description: Code that refers to the autonomous tariff group that is used in the balance.
notes: Examples: BGP: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie BGZ: Behandelingsprovisie bankgarantie BSL: Beslaglegging BVA: Bankverklaring A BVB: Bankverklaring B BVS: Standaardbankverklaring COD: Coulance betaling COU: Coulance betaling CRD: Correctie creditrente CRE: Correctie creditrente DRD: Correctie debetrente DRE: Correctie debetrente KSH: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSL: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSS: Terugboeking schuldsanering KSX: Terugboeking schuldsanering

term_name: Balance Basic Payment Product Number
display_name: Balance Basic Payment Product Number
description: The number of the basic payment product of the agremeent where the balance is related to.
notes: Code (number) indicating a product related to a Basic Payment Agreement (BBO). Also called 'accounttype'

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Begin Date
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Begin Date
description: Date on which the compensation agreement became active.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement End Date
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement End Date
description: Date on which the compensation agreement stops to exist.
notes: The balance compensation agreement is active until 24:00 hours on the end date.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity of balance compensation agreement for registering in the general ledger.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: The code of the operating unit of balance compensation agreement for registering in the general ledger.
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Number
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Number
description: Unique identification of the balance compensation agreement within a branch office.
notes: Balance Compensation is abbreviated as BC. The combination of the attributes Balance Compensation Agreement Office and Balance Compensation Agreement Number are the unique key of the Balance Compensation Agreement.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Office Number
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Office Number
description: Unique identification of the bank branch office maintaining the balance compensation agreement
notes: The branch office number is part of the primary key of the balance compensation agreement.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Currency Code
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Currency Code
description: Currency code of the basic payment agreement participating in the balance compensation agreement.
notes: According to ISO standard 4217 (3 alfanumeric signs).

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement End Datetime
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement End Datetime
description: Date and time on which the participation of the basic payment agreement in the balance compensation agreement ends.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity of the participating basic payment agreement of the balance compensation agreement for registering in the general ledger.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: The code of the operating unit of the participating basic payment agreement of the balance compensation agreement for registering in the general ledger.
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement IBAN
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement IBAN
description: International Bank Account Number of the basic payment agreement participating in the balance compensation agreement.
notes: A basic payment agreement (Basisbetaalovereenkomst, BBO) can at a certain moment be participating in maximally one balance compensation and maximally one interesest compensation.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Maximum Compensation Smallest Unit Amount
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Maximum Compensation Smallest Unit Amount
description: Maximum balance compensation amount for the basic payment agreement, expressed in the smallest unit of the currency.
notes: The maximum amount with which the balance of the account can be (virtually) decreased to increase the balance of another account. The amount is always expressed in the currency of the account. The amount is expressed in the smallest unit of the currency. In case a facility (credit) agreement is present, this can be used additionally, besides the maximum compensation amount, to increase the balance of another account.

term_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Start Datetime
display_name: Balance Compensation Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Start Datetime
description: Start datetime of participation of the basic payment agreement in the balance compensation agreement.

term_name: Balance Credit Management Commission Group Code
display_name: Balance Credit Management Commission Group Code
description: Code that refers to the Credit Management Commission Group that is used in the balance.

term_name: Balance Currency Number Of Decimals
display_name: Balance Currency Number Of Decimals
description: The number of decimals in which the currency of the balance is expressed.
notes: This is based on the currency of the related counterpary account

term_name: Balance Debit Credit Code
display_name: Balance Debit Credit Code
description: Code to indicate whether the balance is a credit or a debit balance.
notes: Possible values: - CRDT = Credit, this means a positive balance for the customer. For the bank this is a debt. - DBIT = Debit, this means a negative balance or balance equal zero for the customer. For the bank this is a benefit.

term_name: Balance Delivery Channel Code
display_name: Balance Delivery Channel Code
description: The code that represents the channel that delivered the balance.
notes: e.g. EXP, FLP, RTI

term_name: Balance Detailed Transaction Type Code
display_name: Balance Detailed Transaction Type Code
description: Code to indicate the detailed type of the financial transaction, related to the balance.
notes: Further detailing of Transaction Type, used among others, for billing. In principle only for internal usage. Example: detailed transaction type cde 23 stands for 'opname geldautomaat niet-' within transaction group 'GA - Geldautomaat'

term_name: Balance General Ledger Bank Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Bank Code
description: The code of the bank where the balance is reported in the general ledger

term_name: Balance General Ledger Bank Location Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Bank Location Code
description: The code of the location within a bank where the balance is related to.
notes: The location code account can be an identifier for: - a location of a local bank - a branch of a local bank - a department within a branch - a special group. The local banks decide for themselves what location codes they want to use. The management of these codes is therefore entirely the responsibility of the local banks and this information is not known to the central systems. The central systems can therefore not carry out any checks on them. The default value is '00'. There is no direct relationship between the location code account and the local bank location.

term_name: Balance General Ledger Date
display_name: Balance General Ledger Date
description: The date for which the balance must be reported in the general ledger.
notes: Date can be a valuedate or a bookdate this depends on the general ledger event.

term_name: Balance General Ledger Event Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Event Code
description: Code that represents the General Ledger balance event.
domain_values: General Ledger event code (Finance is owner)

term_name: Balance General Ledger Event Currency Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Event Currency Code
description: ISO standard 4217 (3 characters) Currency Code of the amount of the general ledger event of a balance.
notes: For some general ledger events the currency of the amount of a balance is different than the currency of the related agreement. The value of the currency code is based on the ISO standard 4217 (3 characters).

term_name: Balance General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity where the balance is reported in the general ledger

term_name: Balance General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: Balance General Ledger Operating Unit Code

term_name: Basic Payment Product Agreement Deviating Disposal Of Authority Code

Based on the priority of these attributes, a final Deviating Disposal of Authority code (with COF action code) is supplied to COF. The COF actioncode is used for authorization of financial transactions. This value is also visible in Siebel. The Deviating Disposal of Authority codes of type 'agreement' are in general used when there is a block on agreement level.

term_name: Basic Payment Product Commission Tariff Table Number
display_name: Basic Payment Product Commission Tariff Table Number

term_name: Basic Payment Product Core Credit Interest Tariff Table Number
display_name: Basic Payment Product Core Credit Interest Tariff Table Number
description: Number of the tariff table that contains the default core credit interest of the basic payment product/currency combination
notes: There are three methods to calculate credit interest: 1. According to the 'Scale system' (Schijfsysteem) 2. According to the 'Step system' (Drempelsysteem) 3. According to the 'Core system' (Kernsysteem)) For the 'Core system', the lowest value-dated balance is determined within the invoice period. The difference between the lowest value-dated balance and '0' is the core. The part of the value-dated balance, per value day within the interest ladder that falls above the core is called the transaction cash. In case of core system, limits are defined separately for core balance and transaction cash amounts.

term_name: Basic Payment Product Cost Settlement Period Number
display_name: Basic Payment Product Cost Settlement Period Number

notes: This determines together with the cost settle period unit code, the length of the cost settlement period. When cost settle period unit code = 'D', the cost settle period number is between 1 en 6. When cost settle period unit code = 'W', the cost settle period number is between 1 en 52. When cost settle period unit code = 'M', then cost settle period number between 1 en 12

term_name: Basic Payment Product Credit Interest Calculation Method Code
display_name: Basic Payment Product Credit Interest Calculation Method Code
description: The code of the default method for calculating the credit interest for a basic payment agreement having the basic payment product/currency combination.
notes: Within CROS the next codes are used: 1 = 30/360, 2 = juist/360, 3 = juist/juist, 4 = 30/juist, 5= 30/365, 6 = juist/365 The first value represent the number of days in the month, the second represents the number of days in the year. Default value: 3

term_name: Basic Payment Product Credit Interest Structure Code
display_name: Basic Payment Product Credit Interest Structure Code
description: The code of the default credit interest structure (scales or steps) for a basic payment agreement having the basic payment product/currency combination.
notes: There are three methods to calculate credit interest: 1. According to the 'Scale system' (Schijfsysteem) 2. According to the 'Step system' (Drempelsysteem) 3. According to the 'Core system' (Kernsysteem)) To calculate credit interest according to the scale system, the credit amount on the account is divided across a number of scales (brackets). Each scale has a percentage. For each part of the credit balance then for the part that falls in a scale, the credit interest amount is calculated according to the percentage for that scale. For credit interest calculation according to the step system then the scale limits as used in the 'scale system' are interpreted as steps (thresholds) rather than scale limits. The spaces between the steps are called classes. For each class, a percentage is determined. For the percentage of the class where the amount falls, the credit interest is calculated for the entire credit balance. The credit interest structure code indicates whether the table with default credit interest tariffs must be interpreted as a table with a scale or a step structure. D Drempel S Schijf

term_name: Credit Interest Structure Bracket Nominal Percentage
display_name: Credit Interest Structure Bracket Nominal Percentage
description: The calculated interest percentage of the bracket of the Credit Interest Structure.
notes: The Floating rate is calculated using the Basic/Minimum/Maximum percentage related attributes. The calculation starts with using the Basic percentage on which an Add/Subtract percentage is applied. After this, the nominal rate is checked to be above the minimum percentage. If not, the minimum percentage is used as nominal rate. If yes, then the nominal rate is checked to be lower than the maximum percentage. If not, the maximum percentage is used as nominal rate. If yes, then rate determined is used as the nominal rate. The Fixed rate is taken from the Fixed Percentage attribute Percentage can be a Negative (-) or Positive (+) rate.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Account Type Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Account Type Code
description: Code of the account type which is debited or credited for a cross border payment order.
domain_values: FXBAL, SUS, XBKBAL, ROFI, FEE, NOS, FLOAT, CUST, LORO
notes: Needed to link the tables which GPP publishes. Example: NOS = Nostro account, LORO = Loro account

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Bank Account Identification
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Bank Account Identification
description: Identification of the credit or debit account of a bank on which the funds are booked in case of a cross border payment order.
notes: Is needed to link the tables which GPP publishes. For a loro account an IBAN and for a nostro account an IBAN or a G-tag (as administrated in the nostro account administration).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Bank Credit Account Identification
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Bank Credit Account Identification
description: Identification of the credit account of a bank on which the funds are booked in case of a cross border payment order.
notes: For a loro account an IBAN and for a nostro account an IBAN or a G-tag (as administrated in the nostro account administration).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Charge Bearer Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Charge Bearer Code
description: The ISO code which indicates the party which bears the charges for the transaction of a cross border payment order.
notes: CRED means all transaction charges are to be borne by the beneficiary customer (=BEN in field 71A Swift message). SHAR means shared charges. Orderning customer pays the charges of his own bank, beneficairy pays charges of his own bank (=SHA in field 71A Swift message). Value DEBT means all charges getting paid by ordening party (both charges own bank as charges beneficiary bank)(=OUR in field 71A Swift message).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Clearing System Acknowledgement Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Clearing System Acknowledgement Code
description: Code that gives information if a payment is accepted by (clearing) system or not.
notes: Value 012 means acknowledgement (Bevestiging), value 019 means not accepted (Niet geaccepteerd).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Account Type Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Account Type Code
description: Code of the account type which is credited for a cross border payment order.
domain_values: FXBAL, SUS, XBKBAL, ROFI, FEE, NOS, FLOAT, CUST, LORO
notes: Needed to link the tables which GPP publishes. Example: NOS = Nostro account, LORO = Loro account

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Currency Amount
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Currency Amount
description: Amount expressed in a specific currency which is booked on the credit account in case of a cross border payment order.
notes: The currency of the payment order can be found in Currency Code.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Country Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Country Code
description: ISO alpha-2 country code of the creditor (beneficiary customer) of a cross border payment
notes: Also for the bank beneficiary (field 58 in the Swift message)

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Payment Method Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Payment Method Code
description: The technical code for the credit payment method in the cross border payment engine on which various internal and external channels for transferring funds are expressed.
notes: Examples are: TARGET2 and EURO1 (for clearing), BOOK (for internal transaction (loro or customer)) and SWIFT (for nostro).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Value Date
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Credit Value Date
description: Credit value date on which an account holder can use the funds based on a cross border payment order

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account Type Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account Type Code
description: Code of the account type which is debited for a cross border payment order.
domain_values: FXBAL, SUS, XBKBAL, ROFI, FEE, NOS, FLOAT, CUST, LORO
notes: Needed to link the tables which GPP publishes. Example: NOS = Nostro account, LORO = Loro account

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Payment Method Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Payment Method Code
description: The technical code for the debit payment method in the cross border payment engine on which various internal and external channels for transferring funds are expressed.
notes: Examples are: TARGET2 and EURO1 (for clearing), BOOK (for internal transaction (loro or customer)) and SWIFT (for nostro).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Value Date
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Debit Value Date
description: Debit value date on which funds on an account holders account are reduced, based on a cross border payment order

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Direction Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Direction Code
description: Code which indicates if it is an outgoing or incoming order in the Cross Border Payment Engine.
notes: I = incoming, O = outgoing

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Final Status Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Final Status Code
description: The final status which the cross border payment order has received in the payment engine.
notes: To distinct processed, rejected and returned payment orders. Example: COMPLETE means processed succesfully.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Foreign Exchange Rate Agreement Flag
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Foreign Exchange Rate Agreement Flag
description: Flag which indicates if for a cross border payment order a foreign exchange rate agreement is used.
notes: 0 = No, 1 = Yes. Required to distinguish this transactions from payment orders where a real time rate is used. Agreed rate is added to the payment order by the operator manualy.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Internal Message Id
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Internal Message Id
description: Internal identification of a Cross Border payment order in the Cross Border Payment Engine.
notes: Is needed to link the tables which GPP publishes.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Message Class Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Message Class Code
description: Code of a classification of a payment order in the Cross Border Payment Engine. It is an internal value based on Swift message type.
domain_values: AF, NAC, OPI, OSN, PAY, PI, SN
notes: Possible values: AF: Anticipated Funds (e.g. Swift MT210) NAC: Non-Accounting OPI: Outgoing Payment Instruction - Direct OSN: Outgoing Settlement Notification - Cover PAY: Payment PI: Incoming Payment Instruction SN: Incoming Settlement Notification

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Payment Engine Final Status Datetime
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Payment Engine Final Status Datetime
description: The date and time on which the cross border payment order has received an end status in the cross border payment engine.
notes: Example: 2016-12-28 15:29:13.817 This means 28th December 2016 with time as hours:minutes:seconds:milliseconds

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Payment Engine Received Datetime
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Payment Engine Received Datetime
description: Moment when cross border payment order enters the cross border payment engine.
notes: Example: 2016-12-28 11:34 This means 28th December 2016 at 11:34 in the morning (24h clock).

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Product Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Product Code
description: Code indicating the product of the cross border payment order.
notes: Can be determined by different systems (based on payment order source). Example WB = World Payment outgoing, EB = Euro Payment outgoing.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Product Reporting Name
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Product Reporting Name
description: A product name meant for reporting on cross border payments.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order bank Customer Credit Account Currency Code
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order bank Customer Credit Account Currency Code
description: The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency of the credit account number of the beneficiary bank customer for a cross border payment order.
notes: Applicable for incoming transactions and for on-us customer payment orders.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order STP Flag
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order STP Flag
description: Flag which indicates whether the cross border transaction had Straight Through Processing (STP), or that it needed manual interaction to get to the endstate.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift Acknowledgement Flag
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift Acknowledgement Flag
description: Flag that gives information if a payment is accepted by Swift or not.
notes: Value 0 means acknowledgement (accepted), value 1 means not accepted.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift End-To-End Reference Id
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift End-To-End Reference Id
description: Unique End-to-End SwiftTransaction Reference (UETR) in case of a cross border payment order which can be processed according to the Swift Global Payment Innovation (GPI) rulebook.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Header Sender BIC
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Header Sender BIC
description: The Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the sender in the header of a Swift message for a cross border payment order

term_name: Cross Border Payment Order Unique External Message Id
display_name: Cross Border Payment Order Unique External Message Id
description: Unique Id refering single message external interaction for a cross border payment order in the cross border payment engine.
notes: Is needed to link the tables which GPP publishes. When a payment, received from channel, is rejected and error mapping is defined in the cross border payment engine, the payment engine converts the internal error code in the payment to an external error code to be sent to channel.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Product Assigned By GPP Flag
display_name: Cross Border Payment Product Assigned By GPP Flag
description: Flag indicating whether or not the product code was assigned by GPP or overwritten in the reporting layer.
notes: Y - Yes, N - No. 2023-05-22, specific for one report an additional cross border product code R2R is determined in the reporting layer instead of the payment engine.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Product Description
display_name: Cross Border Payment Product Description
description: A verbose description of the cross border payment product.

term_name: Cross Border Payment Product End Datetime
display_name: Cross Border Payment Product End Datetime
description: Date and time when product rules for this cross border payment product were decommissioned in GPP.
notes: GPP=Global Pay Plus, the payment engine for cross border payments.

term_name: Currency Code
display_name: Currency Code
description: The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency.

term_name: Currency Smallest Unit Balance
display_name: Currency Smallest Unit Balance
description: The balance represented in the smallest unit of the currency of the related account.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry According To Bank Counterparty Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry According To Bank Counterparty Name
description: Name of the counterparty as known in the bank administration systems, related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Only for internal use of B&S. bank administration systems (i.e. Siebel, CRAB etc.) Counterparty is equal to the Creditor in case of a debit booking and equal to the Debtor in case of a credit booking.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry According To Originator Counterparty Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry According To Originator Counterparty Name
description: Name of the counterparty as provided in the payment order related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Only for internal use of B&S. Counterparty is equal to the creditor in case of a debit booking and equal to the debtor in case of a credit booking.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Generation Number
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Generation Number
description: Generation number of the account related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: The generation number indicates how often an account nummer was issued. This generation number was in use until Februari 2018.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Servicer Reference Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Servicer Reference Id
description: Unique reference as assigned by the account servicer to identify the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: This identification will be assigned always by bank and will be the unique reference for Controlability and Traceability purposes. bank generated ID

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Servicer Transaction Reference Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Account Servicer Transaction Reference Id
description: Unique reference of the transaction as assigned by the account servicer related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: This identification will be assigned always by bank and will be the unique reference for accountability and traceability purposes.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Additional Data Flag
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Additional Data Flag
description: A flag which indicates whether additional data of a current account accounting entry is available.
notes: CNA has multiple booking tables. This attribute is placed in the main booking table where it indicates whether additional data for the accounting entry is available in the CNA or GPS systems.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Account Available Smallest Unit Amount
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Account Available Smallest Unit Amount
description: The amount available of the account after the specific accounting entry is posted in the current account system, expressed in the smallest unit of the currency of the related account.
notes: This field is a signed field.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Available Amount Credit Debit Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Available Amount Credit Debit Code
description: A code that indicates whether the amount available after processing the accounting entry in the current account system is a credit or a debit.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Available Smallest Unit Currency Amount
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry After Processing Available Smallest Unit Currency Amount
description: The amount that is available on a current account after processing the accounting entry, in the smallest unit of the currency of the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Domain Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Domain Code
description: Code used by the bank to indicate the domain area of the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: The domain area indicates the business area of the financial transactions. There are sublevels for each business areas.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Product Family Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Product Family Code
description: Code used by the bank to indicate the product family within the domain area of the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: There are sublevels within family code.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Product Subfamily Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Bank Transaction Product Subfamily Code
description: Code used by the bank to indicate the product subfamily within the family of the domain area of the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Sublevels of family codes.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Batch Flag
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Batch Flag
description: Flag which specifies whether the accounting entry in the current account system is a batch entry or not.
notes: True - Batch booking,False - No batch booking. If it is not a batch entry, it is an individual accounting entry. The amount of a batch entry is the sum of the amounts of all individual transactions.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Batch Number of Transactions
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Batch Number of Transactions
description: The number of transactions within one batch entry in the current account system.
notes: Only applicable to batch entries. Individual bookings will not have a value here.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Rate
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Rate
description: The exchange rate used to convert the source amount to booking amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Rate Text
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Rate Text
description: The exchange rate used to convert the source amount to booking amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system, formatted as text.
notes: Exchange Rate formatted conform ISO20022. The exchange rate contains a maximum of 11 numbers and a decimal point. Of those 11 numbers, maximum 10 decimals can appear after the decimal point. Trailing zeroes are suppressed. If an exchange rate does not have any decimals, the decimal point will not be shown. The exchange rate value is stored adjusted to the left.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Source Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Source Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the currency of the source amount which is exchanged to booking amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Source amount may be the Instructed Amount or the Proprietary Amount, depending if the booking amount has been converted from the instructed amount or the proprietary amount: - From Instructed amount in case of debit entry for Credit Transfer or credit entry for Direct Debit - From Proprietary amount (= interbank settlement amount) for credit entry for Credit Transfer or debit entry for Direct Debit Booking Amount Exchange Source Currency will only be filled in case the related Rate is available.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Target Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Target Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the currency of the target amount i.e. booking amount which is exchanged from the source amount, of the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Booking Amount Exchange Target Currency will only be filled in case the related Rate is available.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Unit Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Booking Amount Exchange Unit Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the unit currency of the exchange rate used for converting the booking amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Booking Amount Exchange Unit Currency will only be filled in case the related Rate is available. For example exchange Rate: 1 EUR = 1,23456 USD. Unit Currency = EUR

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Expiry Date
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the card used in the card transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card PAN
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card PAN
description: The primary account number(PAN) of the card used in the card transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Sequence Number
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Sequence Number
description: Sequence number of the card used in the card transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: This sequence number identifies a card inside a set of cards with the same card number (PAN).

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction POI Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction POI Id
description: Unique identification of the POI for the acquirer accepting the card transactions related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: A unique code identifying a terminal at the card acceptor location. POI = Point Of Interaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction POI Sale Reference Number
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction POI Sale Reference Number
description: Unique reference of the sales as provided by the merchant to the card transaction as recorded at the POI related to the accounting entry.
notes: POI = Point Of Interaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction Reference Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction Reference Id
description: Unique reference id of the card transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: This is assigned by the point of interaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction Transmission Datetime
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Card Transaction Transmission Datetime
description: Date and time expressed in UTC that a card transaction, related to the accounting entry in the current account system, is transmitted.
notes: The date and time will be in UTC format (Coordinated Universal Time). It indicates the date and time that the transaction is entered into the card scheme network for relaying or processing.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry CET Datetime
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry CET Datetime
description: Datetime in CET when the accounting entry is processed in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charge Bearer Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charge Bearer Code
description: Code that specifies which party/parties will bear the charges for processing the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: CRED = Creditor bears all transaction charges, DEBT = Debtor bears all transaction changes, SHAR= debtor and creditor bear the transaction costs on the debtor resp. creditor side, SLEV= Debtor and creditor bear the charges in accordance of the rules agreed in the service level and/or scheme of the transaction. Value in accordance with ISO20022 codeset Priority2Code.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Amount Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Amount Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the currency of the first charges amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Credit Debit Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Credit Debit Code
description: Code that indicates whether the first charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system, is a credit or debit amount.
notes: CRDT = Credit, DBIT = Debit

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Currency Amount
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Currency Amount
description: The amount to be paid by the first charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, expressed in the currency of the account.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers. Hence there will be charges amount for each bearer.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Currency Amount Text
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Currency Amount Text
description: Amount to be paid by the first charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, formatted as text with decimal indicator.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers. Hence there will be charges amount for each bearers.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Address Line1
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Address Line1
description: The first line of text of the address of the party who takes the first transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: First line filled with address data, mostly streetname and house number. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Address Line2
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Address Line2
description: The second line of text of the address of the party who takes the first transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Second line filled with address data, mostly zipcode and place of residence. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party BIC
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party BIC
description: BIC code of the party who takes the first transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BIC code is allocated to organisations by the ISO 9362 Registration Authority under an international identification scheme.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Clearing System Identification Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Clearing System Identification Code
description: Code identifying the clearing system of the party which takes the first charges for the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: The value should be as defined by ISO20022 codeset 'ExternalClearingSystemIdentification1Code'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Clearing System Member Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Clearing System Member Id
description: Member identification of clearing system for the party who takes the first transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: When clearing system member identification is present, clearing system member identification code also needs to be present. E.g. the CHIPS participant identifier of a financial institution that is connected to the US clearing system CHIPS.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Country Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Country Code
description: ISO 2-character country code of the party who takes the first transaction charges or to which the transaction charges are due related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Party Name
description: Name of the party who takes the first transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Type Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges1 Type Code
description: Code that indicates the type of the first charges applicable for processing the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BRKF: Brokerage fee paid to a broker for services provided., COMM: Commission (fee) paid for services provided. Based on ISO20022 External code set 'ExternalChargeType1Code'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Amount Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Amount Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the currency of the second charges amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Credit Debit Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Credit Debit Code
description: Code that indicates whether the second charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system, is a credit or debit amount.
notes: CRDT = Credit, DBIT = Debit. Value in accordance with ISO20022 codeset 'CreditDebitCode'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Currency Amount
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Currency Amount
description: The amount to be paid by the second charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, expressed in the currency of the account.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers. Hence there will be charges amount for each bearer.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Currency Amount Text
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Currency Amount Text
description: Amount to be paid by the second charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, formatted as text with decimal indicator.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers.Hence there will be charges amount for each bearers.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Address Line1
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Address Line1
description: The first line of text of the address of the party who takes the second transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: First line filled with address data, mostly streetname and house number. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Address Line2
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Address Line2
description: The second line of text of the address of the party who takes the second transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Second line filled with address data, mostly zipcode and place of residence. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party BIC
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party BIC
description: BIC code of the party who takes the second transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BIC code is allocated to organisations by the ISO 9362 Registration Authority under an international identification scheme.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Clearing System Identification Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Clearing System Identification Code
description: Code identifying the clearing system of the party which takes the second charges for the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: The value should be as defined by ISO20022 codeset 'ExternalClearingSystemIdentification1Code'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Clearing System Member Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Clearing System Member Id

display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Country Code

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Party Name
description: Name of the party who takes the second transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Type Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges2 Type Code
description: Code that indicates the type of second charges applicable for processing the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BRKF: Brokerage fee paid to a broker for services provided, COMM: Commission (fee) paid for services provided. Based on ISO20022 External code set 'ExternalChargeType1Code'.

display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Party Clearing System Member Id
description: Member identification of clearing system for the party who takes the fourth transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: When clearing system member identification is present, clearing system member Identification code also needs to be present. E.g. the CHIPS participant identifier of a financial institution that is connected to the US clearing system CHIPS.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Party Country Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Party Country Code
description: ISO 2-character country code of the party who takes the fourth transaction charges or to which the transaction charges are due related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Party Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Party Name
description: Name of the party who takes the fourth transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Type Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges4 Type Code
description: Code that indicates the type of fourth charges applicable for processing the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BRKF: Brokerage fee paid to a broker for services provided., COMM: Commission (fee) paid for services provided. Based on ISO20022 External code set 'ExternalChargeType1Code'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Amount Currency Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Amount Currency Code
description: The 3-letter code according to the ISO 4217 standard of the currency of the fifth charges amount related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Credit Debit Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Credit Debit Code
description: Code that indicates whether the fifth charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system, is a credit or debit amount.
notes: CRDT = Credit, DBIT = Debit. Value in accordance with ISO20022 codeset 'CreditDebitCode'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Currency Amount
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Currency Amount
description: The amount to be paid by the fifth charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, expressed in the currency of the account.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers. Hence there will be charges amount for each bearer.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Currency Amount Text
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Currency Amount Text
description: Amount to be paid by the fifth charge bearer for processing the accounting entry in the current account system, formatted as text with decimal indicator.
notes: There could be multiple charge bearers.Hence there will be charges amount for each bearers.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Address Line1
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Address Line1
description: The first line of text of the address of the party who takes the fifth transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: First line filled with address data, mostly streetname and house number. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Address Line2
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Address Line2
description: The second line of text of the address of the party who takes the fifth transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: Second line filled with address data, mostly zipcode and place of residence. Only filled when explicitly mentioned in the payment transaction.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party BIC
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party BIC
description: BIC code of the party who takes the fifth transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: BIC code is allocated to organisations by the ISO 9362 Registration Authority under an international identification scheme.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Clearing System Identification Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Clearing System Identification Code
description: Code identifying the clearing system of the party which takes the fifth charges for the transaction related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: The value should be as defined by ISO20022 codeset 'ExternalClearingSystemIdentification1Code'.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Clearing System Member Id
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Clearing System Member Id
description: Member identification of clearing system for the party who takes the fifth transaction charges related to the accounting entry in the current account system.
notes: When clearing system member identification is present, clearing system member Identification code also needs to be present. E.g. the CHIPS participant identifier of a financial institution that is connected to the US clearing system CHIPS.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Country Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Country Code
description: ISO 2-character country code of the party who takes the fifth transaction charges or to which the transaction charges are due related to the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Name
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Party Name
description: Name of the party who takes the fifth transaction charges for the accounting entry in the current account system.

term_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Type Code
display_name: Current Account Accounting Entry Charges5 Type Code
description: Code that indicates the type of fifth charges applicable for processing the accounting entry in the current account system.
