description: The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency of the amount which is booked on the credit account in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Credit Exchange Rate
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Credit Exchange Rate
description: The price of a currency in terms of another currency on credit side for outgoing cross border payment types.
notes: Needed when currency of the credit account differs from the currency of the transaction. Example: Currency of transation is USD and curreny of account is EUR. Rate can be: 1 EUR = 1,23453 USD.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Account Number
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Account Number
description: Account number of the creditor (end customer) of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Can be IBAN (Inter Bank Account Number) or BBAN (Beneficiairy Bank Account Number).

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the bank of the final creditor (beneficiary) in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent First Address Line
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent First Address Line
description: First Address Line of the bank of the final creditor (beneficiary) in case of an outgoing (pass through) cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent Name
description: Name of the bank of the final creditor (beneficiary) in case of an outgoing (pass through) cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent National Clearing Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent National Clearing Code
description: The numeric code of the national clearing in that country that is associated with creditor agent in case of outgoing cross border payment order or the outgoing customer pass through
notes: Example, ABA number, German BLZ number, and UK Sort Codes. (Swift link naar waardes werkt niet)

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent Second Address Line
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Agent Second Address Line
description: Second address line of the bank of the final creditor (beneficiary) in case of an outgoing (pass through) cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Name
description: Name of the beneficiary in case of an outgoing cross border payment.
notes: Field 59 of an outgoing Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Street Address Line
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Street Address Line
description: Address (street and number) of the creditor (beneficiary) of an outgoing cross border payment.
notes: Field 59 of an outgoing Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Town Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Creditor Town Name
description: Town name of the beneficiary of an outgoing cross border payment.
notes: Field 59 of an outgoing Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Currency Debit Account Booked Amount
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Currency Debit Account Booked Amount
description: Amount which is booked on the debit account for an outgoing cross border payment order expressed in the currency of the debit account.
notes: The currency of the payment order can be found in Currency Code.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account Currency Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account Currency Code
description: The code based on the ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) that identifies the currency of the debit account number of the ordering customer (the debtor) for an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account IBAN
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Account IBAN
description: IBAN of the ordering customer (debtor) of an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Exchange Rate
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debit Exchange Rate
description: The price of a currency in terms of another currency on debit side for outgoing cross border payment types.
notes: Needed when currency of the debit account differs from the currency of the transaction. Example: Currency of transation is USD and curreny of account is EUR. Rate can be: 1 EUR = 1,23453 USD.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Country Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Country Code
description: ISO alpha-2 country code of the debtor (ordering customer) for an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Name
description: Name of the debtor (ordering customer) for an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Postal Code Town Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Postal Code Town Name
description: Postal code and town of the debtor (ordering customer) for an outgoing cross border payment order.
domain_values: No domain values required for 'Postal Code'.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Street Address Line
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Debtor Street Address Line
description: Address (street and number) of the debtor (ordering customer) for an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Intermediary Agent BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Intermediary Agent BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of an intermediary agent in outgoing cross border payment order determined in the payment engine.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Intermediary Agent National Clearing Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Intermediary Agent National Clearing Code
description: The numeric code of the national clearing in that country that is associated with intermediary agent in case of an incoming cross border payment order.
notes: Example, ABA number, German BLZ number, and UK Sort Codes. (Swift link naar waardes werkt niet)

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the bank of the creditor (final beneficiary) delivered by bank customer in the cross border payment order.
notes: Needed to destinguish payment orders with a BIC of the creditor bank and without (= empty) a BIC delivered by a bank Customer. Received in the Payment Engine in pain.001.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent Country Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent Country Code
description: ISO alpha-2 country code of the creditor agent for an outgoing cross border payment order as received in the original payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Creditor Agent Name
description: Name of the bank of the final creditor (beneficiary) delivered by bank customer in the cross border payment order and received in the payment engine via pain.001 message.
notes: The delivered name needed in case a customer didn't deliver a BIC of the creditor agent.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Currency Amount
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Currency Amount
description: Amount denominated in the currency of the transfer) of a cross border payment order initiated by the customer for an outgoing payment order
notes: The instructed amount by the customer. The currency of the payment order can be found in Currency Code.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Currency Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Currency Code
description: The code based on the ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) of the currency of a cross border payment order initiated by the customer for an outgoing payment order.
notes: The instructed currency of the amount.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Intermediary Agent BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Original Intermediary Agent BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of an intermediary agent delivered by bank customer for an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Received in the cross border payment engine in pain.001 file.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Payment Information Id
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Payment Information Id
description: The delivered reference number by feeder system of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Concerns the 'bronreferentienummer' as registered in system OVY.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order bank Delivery Channel Id
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order bank Delivery Channel Id
description: The identification of the channel though which the outgoing cross border payment order is delivered to bank
notes: This value is included in the pain.001 message (tag DlvryChanl)

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order bank Rejection Reason Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order bank Rejection Reason Code
description: ISO code of the reason of rejecting the original outgoing cross border payment order by bank
notes: for external code set names: see ISO20022 Codeset 'ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus1Code', 'ExternalReturnReason1Code', 'ExternalStatusReason1Code'.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Receiver Correspondent Bank BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Receiver Correspondent Bank BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the receiver's correspondent of the receiver of the message as delivered by the sending party in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Field 54 of a Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Reference Id
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Reference Id
description: Unique reference for outgoing cross border payments delivered by Pre-processing or an Order Manager to the cross border payment engine.
notes: This value is included in the pain.001 (tag AcctSvcrRef). This value is stored in the Payment Engine, so the Payment Engine is capable to include this in the outgoing Swift message (for not-on-us). Besides that the Payment Engine creates its own reference.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Related Reference Id
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Related Reference Id
description: Related reference in field 21 of an incoming MT 202 (COV) Swift message with is related to another related message.
notes: Related reference in field 21 of an incoming MT 202 (COV) Swift message with is related to another related message.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Remittance Information Text
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Remittance Information Text
description: Information from ordering customer to beneficiary in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Swift message field 70. This attribute specifies either the details of the individual transaction or a reference to another message containing the details which are to be transmitted to the beneficiary customer.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Charges Currency Amount
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Charges Currency Amount
description: The amount expressed in a specific currency of the transaction charges of an outgoing cross border payment deducted by bank (sending bank) from the transaction amount (charges BEN).
notes: Part of field 71F of an outgoing Swift message. This means a lower interbank settlement amount.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Charges Currency Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Charges Currency Code
description: The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency of the transaction charges of an outgoing cross border payment deducted by bank (sending bank) from the transaction amount (charges BEN).
notes: Part of field 71F of an outgoing Swift message.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Correspondent Bank BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Correspondent Bank BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the sender's correspondent of the cross border payment order as delivered by the sending party in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Field 53 of a Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Correspondent Bank Name
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Sender Correspondent Bank Name
description: Name of the sender's correspondent of the cross border payment order as delivered by the sending party in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.
notes: Field 53 of a Swift Message

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Subchannel Id
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Subchannel Id
description: The identification of the way of delivery of a cross border payment order initiated by bank klant.
notes: E.g. online payment, generic pain, MT101, batch payment order, etc.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Receiver BIC
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Receiver BIC
description: Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the receiver (bank) of the outgoing Swift message in case of an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Text
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Swift Message Text
description: The text of the complete Swift message which bank creates and sends to other bank for an outgoing cross border payment order.

term_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Transfer Method Code
display_name: Outgoing Cross Border Payment Order Transfer Method Code
description: De code of the way of sending the outgoing Swift message. This could be a serial or a direct message with a cover to an intermediary bank.
notes: This could be: S what means Serial, C what means Cover and NULL what means the direct message (MT103) belonging to an MT202COV.

term_name: Payment Account Current Value Currency Balance
display_name: Payment Account Current Value Currency Balance
description: The current value balance of the account in the currency of the account at the moment of the request.

term_name: Payment Description
display_name: Payment Description
description: Description of the payment order as raised by the debtor and is necessary for the beneficiary

term_name: Payment Order Counterparty Bank Account Number
display_name: Payment Order Counterparty Bank Account Number
description: Bank account number of the counterparty of the payment order.
notes: Account number can be present in IBAN- or BBAN format

term_name: Payment Order Counterparty BIC
display_name: Payment Order Counterparty BIC
description: BIC of the bank where the counterparty of the payment order is registered.

term_name: Payment Order Counterparty Customer Name
display_name: Payment Order Counterparty Customer Name
description: Customer name as linked to the counterparty bank account number.

term_name: Payment Order Currency Amount
display_name: Payment Order Currency Amount
description: The amount that will be transferred by processing the payment order, expressed in a specific currency.

term_name: Payment Order Currency Code
display_name: Payment Order Currency Code
description: The code of the currency (ISO char-3) of the amount within the payment order.

term_name: Payment Order Effective Execution Date
display_name: Payment Order Effective Execution Date
description: The actual settlement date of the payment order as determined by bank validation rules.

term_name: Payment Order Id
display_name: Payment Order Id
description: The unique identification for a payment order.
notes: ID is created automatically by the system ROM Online (OO9)

term_name: Payment Order Initiator Bank Account Number
display_name: Payment Order Initiator Bank Account Number
description: Bank account number of the initiator of the payment order.
notes: Account number can be present in IBAN- or BBAN format

term_name: Payment Order Initiator BIC
display_name: Payment Order Initiator BIC
description: BIC of the bank where the initiator of the payment order is registered.

term_name: Payment Order Initiator Customer Name
display_name: Payment Order Initiator Customer Name
description: Customer name as linked to the initiator bank account number.

term_name: Payment Order Product Sub Type Code
display_name: Payment Order Product Sub Type Code
description: Code of the product sub type of the payment order.
notes: Sepa, Cross Border, Instant Payment

term_name: Payment Order Requested Execution Date
display_name: Payment Order Requested Execution Date
description: Requested settlement date as provided by the initiator during the creation of the payment order.

term_name: Related Institution Description
display_name: Related Institution Description
description: The description of the Related Institution.

term_name: Related Institution Id
display_name: Related Institution Id
description: The unique code of institutions / companies (within the bank Group) for which local banks act as an intermediate. E.g.  ‘Interpolis’ and ‘De Lage Landen’.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Bank Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Bank Code
description: The unique identification of the bank office that is administrating the RPK cluster agreement

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Compensation Individual Participating Basic Payment Account Settlement Flag
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Compensation Individual Participating Basic Payment Account Settlement Flag
description: The flag that indicates whether the calculated interest, commission and costs on accounts in a group settlement agreement must first be settled on the individual accounts and then compensated (or the other way around).
notes:
Compensation ('Compensatie'): Posting RPK to individual accounts and then compensate.
Combination ('Combinatie'): RPK directly combine.
'Interest, Commission and Cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Cost Settlement Period Number
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Cost Settlement Period Number
description: Value expressing the number of the cost settlement period for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Cost Settlement Period Unit Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Cost Settlement Period Unit Code
description: Code which indicates the period type which is used for the determination of the length of the settlement period for costs for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: D = day,W = week,M = month. 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Currency Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Currency Code
description: The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency of the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement End Date
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement End Date
description: Last date on which the interest commission cost compensation agreement is / was active.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity of RPK cluster agreement for registering in the general ledger.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: The code of the operating unit of RPK cluster agreement for registering in the general ledger.
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Cost Settlement Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Cost Settlement Code
description: The code that indicates the way in which the RPK (=interest, commissions, costs) is to be settled for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'. Admitted values: 1 = Interest separate, provision separate, costs separate, 2 = Interest separate, provision separate, costs as one amount per settlement period, 3 = Interest separate, provision as one amount, costs separate, 4 = Interest separate, provision as one amount per settlement period.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Settlement Period Number
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Settlement Period Number
description: Value expressing the number of the interest and commission settlement period for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Settlement Period Unit Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Interest Commission Settlement Period Unit Code
description: Code which indicates the period type which is used for the determination of the length of the settlement period for interest and provison for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: D = day,W = week,M = month. 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Invoice Year Start Day Number
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Invoice Year Start Day Number
description: The number of the day that the invoice year starts for the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Invoice Year Start Month Number
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Invoice Year Start Month Number
description: The number of the month in which the invoice year starts for the interest commission cost compensation agreement..
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Number
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Number
description: Unique identification of the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Bank Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Bank Code
description: The unique identification of the bank office that is administering the partcipating basic payment agreement of the RPK cluster agreement
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Commission Settlement Flag
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Commission Settlement Flag
description: The flag that indicates whether the commission of the participating individual basic payment agreements must be included in the group settlement.
notes: Interest, Commission and Cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Cost Settlement Flag
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Cost Settlement Flag
description: The flag that indicates whether the costs of the participating individual basic payment agreements should be included in the group settlement.
notes: Interest, Commission and Cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Currency Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Currency Code
description: Currency code of the basic payment agreement participating in the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'. The ISO Standard 4217 (3 characters) code that identifies the currency of the basic payment agreement.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement End Date
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement End Date
description: Last date on which the account is/was participating in the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: interest commission cost is presented in names as 'RPK'. Participation of the account is up to and including 24:00 hours

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Financial Entity Code
description: The code of the financial entity of the participating basic payment agreement of the RPK cluster agreement for registering in the general ledger.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement General Ledger Operating Unit Code
description: The code of the operating unit of the participating basic payment agreement of the RPK cluster agreement for registering in the general ledger.
domain_values: General Ledger Operating Unit Code (Finance is owner)
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement IBAN
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement IBAN
description: IBAN of the basic payment agreement participating in the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: A basic payment agreement can be participating in maximally one interest commission cost compensation agreement at the same time. 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Interest Settlement Flag
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Interest Settlement Flag
description: The flag that indicates whether the interest rate of the participating individual basic payment agreements should be included in the group settlement.
notes: Interest, Commission and Cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Start Date
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Participating Basic Payment Agreement Start Date
description: Start date of participation of the basic payment agreement in the interest commission cost compensation.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Settlement Account Currency Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Settlement Account Currency Code
description: The currency code of the settlement account of the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Settlement Account IBAN
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Settlement Account IBAN
description: The IBAN of the settlement account on which the interest, costs and provision for the interest commission cost compensation agreement have to be booked from or on.
notes: Account is an active bank account on which settlement is allowed. 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Start Date
display_name: RPK Cluster Agreement Start Date
description: Start date of the interest commission cost compensation agreement.
notes: 'interest commission cost' is presented in names as 'RPK'.

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Base Period Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Base Period Code
description: The code indicating if the credit interest is based on daily value balance or average value balance for credit interest agreement of a RPK Cluster
notes: D rente berekenen over de dagelijkse valutaire saldi,M rente berekenen over het gemiddelde valutaire saldo van een maand

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Calculation Method Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Calculation Method Code
description: The code indicating the calculation method of the credit interest agreement of a RPK Cluster
notes: Within CROS the next codes are used: 1 = 30/360, 2 = juist/360, 3 = juist/juist, 4 = 30/juist, 5= 30/365, 6 = juist/365 The first value represent the number of days in the month, the second represents the number of days in the year. Default value: 3

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Category Bracket Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Category Bracket Code
description: The code indicating the category/bracket of the credit interest agreement of a RPK Cluster
notes: D drempel/category, S schijf/bracket

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Credit Interest Structure Bank Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Credit Interest Structure Bank Code
description: The code of the bank that can use the Credit interest structure for a credit Interest Agreement of a RPK Cluster

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Credit Interest Structure Id
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Credit Interest Structure ID
description: The Id of the Credit Interest Structure for a credit Interest Agreement of a RPK Cluster

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement End Date
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement End Date
description: The end date of the credit interest agreement for a RPK Cluster

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Base Period Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Base Period Code
description: The code indicating if the credit interest is based on daily value balance or average value balance for credit interest agreement month core of a RPK Cluster
notes: D rente berekenen over de dagelijkse valutaire saldi,M rente berekenen over het gemiddelde valutaire saldo van een maand

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Calculation Method Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Calculation Method Code
description: The code indicating the calculation method of the credit interest agreement month core of a RPK Cluster
notes: Within CROS the next codes are used: 1 = 30/360, 2 = juist/360, 3 = juist/juist, 4 = 30/juist, 5= 30/365, 6 = juist/365 The first value represent the number of days in the month, the second represents the number of days in the year. Default value: 3

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Category Bracket Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Category Bracket Code
description: The code indicating the category/bracket of the credit interest agreement month core of a RPK Cluster
notes: D drempel/category, S schijf/bracket

term_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Credit Interest Structure Bank Code
display_name: RPK Cluster Credit Interest Agreement Month Core Credit Interest Structure Bank Code
description: The code of the bank that can use the Credit interest structure for a credit Interest agreement month core of a RPK Cluster

I.e. as from a batch containing 10 transactions eventually 6 transactions must be sent to Equens then these will be put in 1 OPIC and the other 4 will be processed internally, then these will be put together in another OPIC. OPIC = Outgoing Payment Instruction Chunk

notes: 'SDDCORE' = SEPA Direct Debit Core, 'SDDB2B' = SEPA Direct Debit Business to Business, 'SCT' = SEPA Credit Transfer

display_name: SEPA Transaction Proprietary Category Purpose Code

term_name: SEPA Transaction Proprietary Local Instrument Code

notes: This id will be passed on unchanged in the interbank chain. The first bank via which the transaction is delivered by the initiator will determine this id. Transactions can be processed via more than one partnerbanks.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Refund Flag

notes: T = refund, F = no refund

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Flag
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Flag

notes: T = R-Transaction, F = No R-transaction R-transaction is a transaction as a result of a rejection

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Id
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Id

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Level Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Level Code

notes: An R-Transaction that is a normal transaction has level 1. An R-Transaction that is another R-Transaction with level n has the level n+1.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Post Settlement Flag
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Post Settlement Flag

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Qualification Type Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Qualification Type Code
description: The code for a SEPA R-Transaction, that specifies the qualification.
domain_values: Cancel, RejectedValueDateCorrection, RecallInquiry, Cancellation, Resolution, ClaimValueDateCorrectionInquiry, Return, ClaimNonReceipt, RejectedClaimNonReceipt, ConfirmedValueDateCorrection, Reversal, Refund, AcceptedClaimNonReceipt, ClaimValueDateCorrection, ClaimNonReceiptInquiry, Refusal, Rejection
notes: Rejection, Return, Refund, Refusal, etc Code is a sub-classification that allows a more fine-grained distinction between the different R-Transactions

term_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Reason Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Reject Reason Code
description: The code for the functional description of the reason for the rejection on SEPA transaction level
notes: ISO20022 Codeset 'CancellationReason5Code'.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Sending Channel Id
display_name: SEPA Transaction Sending Channel Id
description: The identification of the channel of origin who sends the SEPA transaction to the payment engine

term_name: SEPA Transaction Sending System Reference Id
display_name: SEPA Transaction Sending System Reference Id
description: The identifier of the Transaction given by the instructor and passed on by the Instructing Agent, and that should be unique per Instructing agent

term_name: SEPA Transaction Sequence Type Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Sequence Type Code
description: The code of the type of mandate that identifies the direct debit sequence.
notes: FRST (first), RCUR (recurrent), FNAL (final), OOFF (one-off).

term_name: SEPA Transaction Service Level Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Service Level Code
description: The code for the rules under which the SEPA transaction should be processed.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Servicing Office Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Servicing Office Code
description: The code of the branch office where the SEPA transaction is initiated

term_name: SEPA Transaction Settlement Date
display_name: SEPA Transaction Settlement Date
description: The date on which the funds are exchanged between the bank and the destination Participant.
notes: Note that if the incoming settlement day is not the same as the outgoing settlement day, this attribute must contain the later, while incomingSettlementDay contains the former.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Status Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Status Code
description: The code of the actual system status of the SEPA transaction
notes: ''Processed'',''Cancelled',''Created'', 'Warehoused',

term_name: SEPA Transaction Status Reason Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Status Reason Code
description: The code for the functional description of the reason for the status on SEPA transaction level
notes: ISO20022 Codeset 'CancellationReason5Code'.

term_name: SEPA Transaction Structured Description
display_name: SEPA Transaction Structured Description
description: The description of the SEPA transaction in a structured way

term_name: SEPA Transaction Structured Remittance Information Reference Type Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction Structured Remittance Information Reference Type Code
description: The code of the reference type of the remittance information of the SEPA transaction
notes: If filled it should be 'CUR'

term_name: SEPA Transaction System Status Code
display_name: SEPA Transaction System Status Code
description: The code of the system status of the SEPA transaction (at moment of selection) as being sent to the channel
domain_values: e.g. Sent to Bank, Rejected by Bank, Awaiting Authorisation
notes: I.e. .Sent to Bank, Rejected by Bank, Awaiting Authorisation, ...

term_name: SEPA Transaction Transfer Own Account Flag
display_name: SEPA Transaction Transfer Own Account Flag
description: The flag indicating for a SEPA Transaction that it is containing a payment crediting an account owned by the debtor
notes: J = Transfer to own account, N = Transfer to account of another person i.e. a transaction from current account to savings acoount

term_name: SEPA Transaction Ultimate Creditor Name
display_name: SEPA Transaction Ultimate Creditor Name
description: The name of the creditor at the beginning or the end of the SEPA transaction if the transaction uses multiple steps.
notes: Will be empty for SEPA

term_name: SEPA Transaction Ultimate Debtor Name
display_name: SEPA Transaction Ultimate Debtor Name
description: The name of the debtor at the beginning or the end of the SEPA transaction if the transaction uses multiple steps.
notes: Will be empty for SEPA

term_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Description
display_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Description
description: The description of the SEPA transaction in a unstructured way

term_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 1
display_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 1
description: The text of the first line of the description of the SEPA transaction in a unstructured way

term_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 2
display_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 2
description: The text of the second line of the description of the SEPA transaction in a unstructured way

term_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 3
display_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 3
description: The text of the third line of the description of the SEPA transaction in a unstructured way

term_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 4
display_name: SEPA Transaction Unstructured Remittance Information Line 4
description: The text of the fourth line of the description of the SEPA transaction in a unstructured way

term_name: Settlement Account IBAN
display_name: Settlement Account IBAN
description: The IBAN accountnumber on which the interest, costs and provision have to be booked from or on.

term_name: Settlement Account Number Currency Code
display_name: Settlement Account Number Currency Code
description: The currency of the settlement account number.

term_name: Start Invoice Year Day Number
display_name: Start Invoice Year Day Number
description: The number of the day that the invoice year starts.

term_name: Start Invoice Year Month Number
display_name: Start Invoice Year Month Number
description: The number of the month in which the invoice year starts.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Elements Code
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Elements Code
description: A code consisting of the different components in the correct order of an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.
notes: The purpose of the Elements Code is simply to explain which components compose the account number or the national ID.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Local Name
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Local Name
description: Name for an account number in the local language in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Maximum Number of Characters
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Maximum Number of Characters
description: The maximum number of characters used in an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Minimum Number of Characters
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Minimum Number of Characters
description: The minimum number of characters used in an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Type Code
description: A code that indicates the (format) type of the account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file .
notes: A country might have multiple types/kinds of account numbers. This field lists the types. Some are generic, some are country-specific (which occur only once) and some are bank-specific. The types are as follows: - Account Number - Account Number - [bank name] For example: Account number - STATE BANK OF INDIA - Account number - [application area] For example: Account number - Interbank payment - IBAN - Fedwire

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Without Separators Example Value
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number Without Separators Example Value
description: Example value with no separators of an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number With Separators Example Value
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Account Number With Separators Example Value
description: Example value with separators of an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Comment
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Comment
description: Commentary text field used for any other comments for users of the SWIFT Account Number Format file.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country in which a certain account number format in the SWIFT Account Number Format file originates.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country in which the account number format in the SWIFT Account Number Format file originates.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Letter Format Account Number
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Letter Format Account Number
description: An account number in letter format in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.
notes: Approximation of the account number format using letters. The letter format is not able to express different options in case they exist.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Account Number Format file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Record Id
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format Record Id
description: Unique meaningless identification of a record in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.
notes: All Record Id's start with 'AF'. This field is called 'Record Key' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Account Number Format REGEX Format Account Number
display_name: SWIFT Account Number Format REGEX Format Account Number
description: An account number in REGEX (Regular Expression) format of an account number in the SWIFT Account Number Format file.
notes: The regular expression is a formal way of specifying the structure of a data element.

term_name: SWIFT AM Monthly New BIC
display_name: SWIFT AM Monthly New BIC
description: The new unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) related to the institution from the BIC Directory 2018, as found in the SWIFT AM Monthly file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office)

term_name: SWIFT AM Monthly Old BIC
display_name: SWIFT AM Monthly Old BIC
description: The old unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) related to the institution from the BIC Directory 2018, as found in the SWIFT AM Monthly file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office)

term_name: SWIFT AM Monthly Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT AM Monthly Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT AM Monthly file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the AM Monthly file this Tag is always 'AM'.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus BIC
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus BIC
description: The unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) related to the institution from the BIC Directory 2018, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office)

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus BIC8
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus BIC8
description: The alpha-8 bank identifier code (BIC) of the institution/branch as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: BIC (party prefix, country, and party suffix) where: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char)

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 1
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 1
description: The 1st (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 2
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 2
description: The 2nd (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 3
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 3
description: The 3rd (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 4
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Address Line 4
description: The 4th (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch BIC
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch BIC
description: The alpha-3 bank identifier code (BIC) of the branch associated with the 8-character BIC, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: If no branch identifier exists, then XXX is used.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch City Name
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch City Name
description: The name of the city where the institution/branch is located, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch CPS Name
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch CPS Name
description: The name of the county, province, state or other administartive region where the institution/branch is located, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Information Text
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Information Text
description: Text field showing Branch information derived from the BIC Directory 2018 as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: If the branch information is blank in the BIC Directory 2018 and the respective BIC is linked to a National ID with available branch information, then it will be populated in the Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 name for the country in which the institution/branch resides as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country in which the institution/branch resides as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch PO BOX Number
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch PO BOX Number
description: The post office box number of the institution/branch and the related ZIP code, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: Example 'POB 103352, 40024' This allows customers to use the full POB address: POB 103352 40024, DUESSELDORF (CITY).

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Qualifier Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Qualifier Code
description: Code consisting of one or multiple three character codes indicating the branch qualifier(s) (also called operational type(s)) of the entity, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
domain_values: ADM = Administration,ALL = All Branches,BKO = Back Office,BRA = Branch,CLS = Continuous Linked Settlement,CMI = Cash Management & Customer Status,COL = Collections & Cash Letters,COM = Communications,DOC = Documentary Credits & Guarantees,FEX = Foreign Exchange,FIT = Financial Institution Transfers,FND = Investment Funds,GEB = General Banking,HDO = Head Office,MGT = Management,MMD = Money Markets & Derivatives,ORO = Order Routing,PAY = Customer Payments & Cheque,PME = Precious Metals,SMK = Securities Markets,STY = Security,SYN = Syndications,TMK = Treasury Markets
notes: Branch Qualifiers are provided only for records containing a BIC. The BRANCH QUALIFIERS field can contain up to 6 three-character branch qualifiers in alphabetic order.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Subtype Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Subtype Code
description: Code indicating the branch subtype (als called business type or user category) of the entity, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: This field is called 'Subtype Indicator' by SWIFT. Subtype Indicator is provided only for records with a BIC.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Timezone Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch Timezone Code
description: Code indicating the timezone of the institution/branch (formally the location code in position 7 of BIC standard:2009) as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: Examples In FR, BICs in Paris = [P] In BE, BICs in Brussels = [B] In US, BICs on East Cost = [3]

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ZIP Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Branch ZIP Code
description: The zip code belonging to the place where the institution/branch is located, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus CHIPS UID
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus CHIPS UID
description: The 6 digit Clearing House Interbank Payments System (CHIPS) Universal ID related to the institution as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Connected BIC
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Connected BIC
description: The connected BIC of the same institution, if available, or of its correspondent through which it connects, for a BIC that is not connected to the SWIFT network as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Cooperative Group Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Cooperative Group Id
description: Unique identifier of the record of the cooperative central bank of a group if the record indicates a cooperative bank which belongs to a cooperative bank grouping, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: Hierarchy in this case flows downward. In the case of any such cooperative central bank, the value here is its own Record_Key. If the cooperative bank concerned does not belong to a cooperative bank grouping, then the field is empty.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Group Parent Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Group Parent Id
description: Unique identifier of the record of the parent entity belonging to the group in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: This value identifies the set of entities (records) belonging to the group. It groups BICs and National ID records that belong to the same parent (parent BIC).

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Group Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Group Type Code
description: Code indicating the type of entity that identifies the group in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: This data comes from the SWIFT back office. The parent BIC is the BIC at the top of SWIFT traffic hierarchy. This can be different than the legal ultimate parent that is available in Entity Plus.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Head Office Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Head Office Id
description: Unique identifier of the record of the highest entity upwards (the Head Office) in the entities hierarchy in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus IBAN Key Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus IBAN Key Id
description: Unique identifier of the record in the IBANPLUS txt file that contains the IBAN data for this entity, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Institution Name
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Institution Name
description: The trading name of the institution as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Institution Status Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Institution Status Code
description: Code indicating the licence status of the institution (if any) as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
domain_values: BANK = Universal Bank,COOP = Cooperative Bank,CBMA = Central Bank/Monetary Authority,ASSM = Asset manager,CORP = Corporates,CRUN = Credit Union,ICSD = International Central Securities Depository,INVM = Investment Bank,MONX = Money Exchange,NPAA = National payment association,PYMI = Payments Institution,SLBH = Saving-Lending Bank Housing,SUFI = Supervised Financial Institution,XCHG = Exchange,OTHR = Other

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus ISO LEI Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus ISO LEI Code
description: Code for the 20-digit Legal Entity Identifier according to ISO 17442 universal standard used for the unique identification of legal entities involved in financial transactions, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: LEI is used for the unique identification of legal entities involved in financial transactions. This information is provided in BDP for entities with a BIC. The full list of LEIs is provided in Reach Plus (another SWIFTRef product).

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Legal Parent Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Legal Parent Id
description: Unique identifier of the record of the closest legal parent entity (L) upwards in the legal hierarchy in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Legal Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Legal Type Code
description: Code indicating the legal type of the entity in the legal hierarchy in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
domain_values: L = Legal Entity,B = Business Entity,U = Unknown
notes: In this context, only those entities which are classified in field 'Institution Status Code' as being one of the following are flagged as 'L' or 'B': BANK = Universal Bank COOP = Cooperative Bank CBMA = Central Bank/Monetary Authority CRUN = Credit Union PYMI = Payments Institution XCHG = Exchange All 'OTHR' Other are flagged as 'U'.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus National Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus National Id
description: The National identifier of the institution/branch as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus National Id Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus National Id Type Code
description: Code indicating the National ID Type and additional coding of an entity, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: '- National ID Type This is the name of the national code or the name/acronym of the national code provider. Examples: BLZ for the German NATIONAL ID ABA for the US NATIONAL ID - National ID Type + /AN AN stand for Alternative National ID Type. The banks/branches in a country can own National IDs issued by multiple authorities. For example, banks in Brazil can own COMPE national IDs and ISPB National IDs. The second code is indicated as ISPB/AN. - National ID Type + /FN FN stands for Foreign National ID Type. In some countries, banks have a National code issued by a foreign payment clearing service, such as a German bank that has a Swiss BC. This is indicated as BC/FN.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Network Connectivity Status Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Network Connectivity Status Code
description: Code consisting of three characters indicating the status of an entity's connection to the following three SWIFT networks: FIN, Interact and Fileact, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
domain_values: CCC, CCN, CNC, CNN, NNC, NCN, NCC, NNN
notes: C = Connected N = Not connected Example: For an institution connected to FIN and FileAct but not to InterAct = CNC

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Office Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Office Type Code
description: Code indicating the office type according to the entities hierarchy of the entity in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
domain_values: HO - Head Office,MP - Main Payments Office,DB - Domestic Branch or department,SB - Sub Domestic Branch,FB - Foreign Branch,SF - Sub Foreign Branch,UC - Unclassified

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Parent Office Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Parent Office Id
description: Unique identifier of the record of the closest entity upwards in the entities hierarchy in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: For example, the 'HO' RECORD KEY if the entity is 'DB', or the 'FB' RECORD KEY if it is 'SF'.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Record Id
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Record Id
description: Unique identification of a record in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: All Record Id's start with 'BD'. This field is called 'Record Key' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Service Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Service Code
description: Code consisting of one or multiple 3 Character codes which indicate one or more SWIFT Service(s) in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: Service Codes are provided only for records containing a BIC. The field can contain up to 20 three-character codes in alphabetical order.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus SSI Group Code
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus SSI Group Code
description: Code indicating to which SSI Group the entity belongs in the SSI Plus files, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: This field is called 'SSI Group Key' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Valid From Date
display_name: SWIFT Bank Directory Plus Valid From Date
description: The valid-from date of the whole record due to a change to its attributes, as found in the SWIFT Bank Directory Plus file.
notes: Examples: BIC activated/de-activated in the future NATIONAL ID valid as from a future date Contains date in format YYYYMMDD. The field is empty in case the record is valid at the date specified in the file name (that is, the publication date).

term_name: SWIFT Bank SEPA Routing BIC
display_name: SWIFT Bank SEPA Routing BIC
description: The unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) of the financial institution of the beneficiary, as found in the SWIFT SEPA Routing file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office)

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 BIC
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 BIC
description: The alpha-8 bank identifier code (BIC) of the institution/branch as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.
notes: BIC (party prefix, country, and party suffix) where: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char)

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 1
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 1
description: The 1st (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 2
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 2
description: The 2nd (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 3
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 3
description: The 3rd (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 4
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Address Line 4
description: The 4th (of 4) address line of the institution/branch which might contain the building name, detailed building information, street name, and number, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch BIC
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch BIC
description: The alpha-3 bank identifier code (BIC) of the branch associated with the 8-character BIC, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.
notes: If no branch identifier exists, then XXX is used.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch City Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch City Name
description: The name of the city where the institution/branch is located, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Information Text
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Information Text
description: Text field showing branch information as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Subtype Code
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Branch Subtype Code
description: Code indicating the branch subtype (als called business type or user category) of the entity, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.
notes: This field is called 'Subtype Indicator' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Extra Information Code List
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Extra Information Code List
description: The list of codes indicating extra information on services subscriptions of the financial institution, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.
notes: Positions: 1-18: can contain up to 6 three-character branch qualifiers in alphabetic order. Do not put spaces between the codes. 19: contains the SWIFTNet FIN connectivity status 20: contains location code information - alphanumeric 21-35: are unused and left empty

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Institution Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Institution Name
description: The trading name of the institution as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country in which the institution/branch resides, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Location Full Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Location Full Name
description: The full name of the location (city, postal code, county, province, state, or other administrative region) of the institution/branch, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Country Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country of the post office box of the institution/branch, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Location Name
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Location Name
description: The location of the post office box of the institution/branch as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Number
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 PO Box Number
description: The post office box number of the institution/branch and the related ZIP code, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the BIC Directory 2018 file this Tag is always 'FI'.

term_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Value Added Services Code List
display_name: SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 Value Added Services Code List
description: The list of codes for the value-added services that the financial institution has subscribed to, as found in the SWIFT BIC Directory 2018 file.
notes: Positions: 1-48: can contain up to 16 three-character service codes in alphabetical order. 49-51: can contain the preferred service code. If not used, they are left blank. 52-60: are reserved for future use. Do not put spaces between the codes. A plus sign (+) as the third character indicates another participant of this service.

term_name: SWIFT Country Code ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Country Code ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country as found in the SWIFT Country Code file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Country Code ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT Country Code ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country as found in the SWIFT Country Code file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Country Code Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Country Code Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Country Code file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Country Code Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT Country Code Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT Country Code file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the Country Code file this Tag is always 'CT'.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country belonging to the ISO 4271 currency, as found in the SWIFT Currency Code file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country belonging to the ISO 4271 currency, as found in the SWIFT Currency Code file.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Currency Code
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Currency Code
description: The ISO 4217 alpha-3 code for the currency as found in the SWIFT Currency Code file.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Currency Name
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code ISO Currency Name
description: The ISO 4217 name of the currency as found in the SWIFT Currency Code file.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Currency Code file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code Number Of Fractional Digits
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code Number Of Fractional Digits
description: The number of fractional digits of the currency according to ISO 4217, as found in the SWIFT Currency Code file.

term_name: SWIFT Currency Code Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT Currency Code Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT Currency Code file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the Currency Code file this Tag is always 'CU'.

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List BIC
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List BIC
description: The unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) related to the institution that belongs to the national id, as found in the SWIFT Exclusion List file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office)

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List IBAN National Id
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List IBAN National Id
description: The national id that, if included in an IBAN, would lead to an invalid IBAN, as found in the SWIFT Exclusion List file.
notes: The IBAN NATIONAL ID has the country-specific length as specified in the IBANSTRUCTURE_V2 file.

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Invalid From Date
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Invalid From Date
description: The future date from which the IBAN NATIONAL ID will be invalid, as found in the SWIFT Exclusion List file.
notes: If the IBAN NATIONAL ID became invalid in the past, then the field is empty.

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country in which the National Id's are used, as found in the SWIFT Exclusion List file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Exclusion List file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Record Id
display_name: SWIFT Exclusion List Record Id
description: Unique meaningless identification of a record in the SWIFT Exclusion List file.
notes: All Record Id's start with 'EL'. This field is called 'Record Key' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Date
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Date
description: The date on which a holiday in the SWIFT Holiday file takes place.
notes: Format is YYYYMMDD

term_name: SWIFT Holiday ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country in which the holiday in the SWIFT Holiday file takes place.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT Holiday ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT Holiday ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country in which the holiday in the SWIFT Holiday file takes place.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Holiday file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Date
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Date
description: The date on which a holiday in the SWIFT Holiday Services file takes place.
notes: Format is YYYYMMDD

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT Holiday Services file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Service Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Service Code
description: A 3 Character code which indicates a SWIFT Service in the SWIFT Holiday Services file.
notes: Also called value added service code (VAS code). In the Holiday Services file the value is always 'EUR'.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Special Information Text
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Special Information Text
description: Text field which states the restrictions that apply if a holiday in the SWIFT Holiday Services file is not applicable countrywide, or is not a full day, or additional text for all holiday types.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT Holiday Services file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the Holiday Services file this Tag is always 'HS'.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Services Type Code
description: A code that indicates the type of holiday in the SWIFT Holiday Services file .
domain_values: A = Morning weekend holiday (occurring every week),D = Special exchange holiday in country,E = Exchange holidays,F = Morning exchange holidays,G = Afternoon exchange holidays,H = Normal holiday,P = Afternoon weekend holiday (occurring every week),S = Special holiday,W = Weekend holiday,X = Morning holiday (occurring only once),Y = Afternoon holiday (occurring only once)

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Special Information Text
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Special Information Text
description: Text field which states the restrictions that apply if a holiday in the SWIFT Holiday file is not applicable countrywide, or is not a full day, or additional text for all holiday types.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Tag Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Tag Code
description: Code used to tag the specific SWIFT Holiday file in SWIFT Reference data.
notes: For the Holiday file this Tag is always 'HF'.

term_name: SWIFT Holiday Type Code
display_name: SWIFT Holiday Type Code
description: A code that indicates the type of holiday in the SWIFT Holiday file .
domain_values: A = Morning weekend holiday (occurring every week),D = Special exchange holiday in country,E = Exchange holidays,F = Morning exchange holidays,G = Afternoon exchange holidays,H = Normal holiday,P = Afternoon weekend holiday (occurring every week),S = Special holiday,W = Weekend holiday,X = Morning holiday (occurring only once),Y = Afternoon holiday (occurring only once)

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN BIC
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN BIC
description: The unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) that has been issued together with the clients' IBANs, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: The BIC consists of: party prefix (4 char) country code (2 char) party suffix (2 char) branch identifier (3 char - XXX for main office) If an 8-character BIC was issued, then it is padded with 'XXX'.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code prefix of the IBANs that the institution issued, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: Under certain circumstances, this can be different from the country indicated in field 'ISO Country Code' or from the country code within the IBAN BIC. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN National Id
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus IBAN National Id
description: The national id that is included in an IBAN, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Institution Name
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Institution Name
description: The trading name of the institution that has issued the IBAN as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus ISO Country Code
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus ISO Country Code
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country in which the institute that has issued the IBAN resides, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus ISO Country Name
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus ISO Country Name
description: The ISO 3166-1 name of the country in which the insitute resides that has issued the IBAN, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Modification Code
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Modification Code
description: Code indicating the update status of a specific record in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file compared to the previous (monthly or daily) publication.
notes: This field is called 'Modification Flag' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Record Id
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Record Id
description: Unique meaningless identification of a record in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: All Record Id's start with 'IB'. This field is called 'Record Key' by SWIFT.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Routing BIC
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Routing BIC
description: The unique alpha-11 bank identifier code (BIC) that is not related to SEPA (ISO 20022 XML) payments, as found in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: The ROUTING BIC can be applied to non-SEPA IBAN payments to find the following information: the SWIFT-connected correspondent of an unconnected bank the central payments processing address of a bank with many unconnected branch BICs or with multiple BICs due to take-overs and mergers.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Service Context Code
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Plus Service Context Code
description: Code indicating the context of the payment services provided by the financial institution SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area), in the SWIFT IBAN Plus file.
notes: Where this is not indicated, the institution uses the IBAN standard but is not a participant of SEPA.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Structure IBAN Account Number Number Of Characters
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Structure IBAN Account Number Number Of Characters
description: The number of characters used in an account number in IBAN, in the SWIFT IBAN Structure file.

term_name: SWIFT IBAN Structure IBAN Account Number Position Number
display_name: SWIFT IBAN Structure IBAN Account Number Position Number
